hero.title: Building a continuous, ever-growing standard of education in Africa
hero.summary: Santos Creations Educational Foundation (SCEF) is a membership-driven Pan-African education foundation delivering Education for All across Africa's five regions (North, West, Central, East, Southern), with strategic support from the African diaspora (sixth region) through trusted partnerships, local chapters, and digital delivery systems.
hero.imageAlt: African students in classroom learning together

expanded.p1: SCEF operates through layered governance, local chapters, digital learning, TVET workforce readiness, inclusive education, and transparent partnerships—designed for measurable outcomes across countries and regions.
expanded.p2: SCEF also provides CSR for Education Funds Management Services (SCEF–FMS), helping institutions deploy education CSR budgets through verified project selection and approval, milestone-based disbursement tracking, ESG/SDG-aligned reporting, and quarterly outcome measurement, supported by dashboards and audit-ready documentation.
expanded.p3: SCEF positions as an NGO delivery and accountability partner for multi-year (minimum 10-year renewable) education partnerships with institutions such as the African Union, development partners, corporate foundations, and global education funds—supporting scalable implementation aligned with SDG 4 and AU Agenda 2063.

hero.csrHelper: Deploy education CSR funds through verified projects, tracked disbursement, and measurable impact reporting.

trust.title: Partner-ready delivery and accountability
trust.point1: Verified education project pipeline and milestone plans
trust.point3: Quarterly outcome reporting aligned to ESG/SDG indicators
trust.point4: Chapter-based delivery model for scalable implementation

hero.title: About Santos Creations Educational Foundation (SCEF)
hero.subtitle: SCEF is a membership-driven Pan-African education foundation strengthening education delivery through governance systems, digital access and certification, media knowledge dissemination, compliant local chapters, accountable funding partnerships, and scalable impact aligned with the UN SDGs and AU Agenda 2063.
hero.tagline: Building a continuous, ever-growing standard of education in Africa.
hero.description: SCEF is a membership-driven Pan-African education foundation advancing Education for All through governance, local chapters, digital learning, TVET workforce readiness, inclusive education, and transparent partnerships.
hero.trustLine: Founded 1997 • Registered 2010 • SDG 4 + AU Agenda 2063 aligned • HQ-led governance + chapter-driven delivery

expanded.p1: Santos Creations Educational Foundation (SCEF) is a membership-based, not-for-profit and membership-driven Pan-African education foundation advocating for transforming education across Nigeria and Africa through sustainable practices, innovation, and community-driven initiatives.
expanded.p2: We also provide CSR for Education Funds Management Services—supporting corporations and institutions to deploy education CSR funds through verified projects, tracked disbursement, measurable outcomes, and accountability reporting aligned with sustainability and ESG expectations.
expanded.p3: In addition, SCEF delivers Africa-recognised stakeholder and African-institution endorsed certification/endorsement services to validate education quality efforts, strengthen credibility, and support continuous improvement across schools, programs, and partner interventions.

hero.imageAlt: Learners on the journey to school and an inclusive TVET classroom supporting practical skills and special needs learning.
hero.joinChapterHelper: Join an online local chapter of your choice and country — as a resident, diaspora, or friend of the country.

who.title: A Structured Education Impact Ecosystem
who.body: Santos Creations Educational Foundation (SCEF) is a structured education impact ecosystem—built to help education systems improve, scale, and stay accountable. We connect local chapters, partners, digital platforms, and media advocacy to deliver measurable outcomes in:

whyWeExist.title: Why We Exist
whyWeExist.intro: Across Africa, learning is still limited by:
challenges: Poor access to skills training and TVET labs
Gaps in inclusion for learners with disabilities
Limited pathways from learning to decent work
whyWeExist.solutionDesc: —by building a governance-backed, chapter-driven delivery model that makes education transformation measurable, inclusive, and scalable.

whatWeDo.title: What We Do
whatWeDo.intro: Featured Programs designed to work together on access, quality, inclusion, skills, and recognition.

tvetSpotlight.title: TVET, Skills, and Inclusive Education: Practical Learning for Every Learner
tvetSpotlight.intro: We believe education must prepare learners for life—through skills, confidence, and opportunity, not only certificates. SCEF supports TVET-aligned pathways and inclusive learning environments that empower:
empowerPoints: Learners building real-world skills (technical, digital, vocational)
Students in special needs settings accessing supportive tools
Communities creating practical learning hubs linked to local opportunity
tvetSpotlight.tvetSupportDesc: starter equipment, training linkages, digital skills certification pathways
tvetSpotlight.inclusiveAccessDesc: advocacy for learning aids, accessible content, inclusive teacher support
tvetSpotlight.outcomesFocusDesc: employability skills, entrepreneurship readiness, community value creation
tvetSpotlight.imageAlt: TVET workshop learners practicing hands-on skills alongside an inclusive classroom supporting a special needs learner.

whatWeDo.title: What We Do
whatWeDo.intro: Featured Programs designed to work together on access, quality, inclusion, skills, and recognition.

nesaTv.desc: Education media, stories, and live coverage

objectives.title: Our 6 Core Objectives
objectives.subtitle: Our mission is delivered through six strategic objectives with clear delivery tools and measurable outcomes.
items: To build the platform for advocating a continuous, ever-growing standard of education in Africa—through standards frameworks, benchmarking, recognition, and endorsed certification/verification systems.
To expand equitable access to learning and certification by deploying integrated digital platforms for resources, skills, and verifiable outcomes across Africa and the diaspora.
To strengthen education advocacy and knowledge dissemination by developing media channels (TV, radio, webinars, podcasts, publications) that promote adoption of quality and inclusion practices.
To establish and monitor compliant delivery networks by onboarding, supporting, and auditing local chapters (physical and online) to implement programs with measurable reporting.

To mobilize and manage education funding with accountability—including CSR for Education Funds Management Services—using verified project pipelines, tracked disbursement, and ESG/SDG-aligned reporting.

To scale Education for All across Africa's five regions (North, West, Central, East, Southern) with support from a sixth region: the African diaspora, by building renewable multi-year partnerships with institutions, multilaterals, and global education funders.

divisionsSection.title: How SCEF Works (Six Divisions)
divisionsSection.subtitle: Our operational structure ensures coordinated delivery of programs, governance, and stakeholder engagement.

impact.title: Impact & Accountability
impact.subtitle: Transparent reporting and measurable outcomes for all stakeholders.
impact.frameworkDesc: We report impact through governance reporting, program delivery records, chapter reporting systems, and partner-ready documentation:
impact.reportingDesc: Live impact metrics and regional data are being consolidated. Full reporting dashboard coming soon.

sustainability.title: Sustainability & Alignment
sustainability.body: SCEF aligns programs with SDG 4 and broader SDG outcomes, and supports Africa-wide scalability through AU Agenda 2063. Our approach prioritizes:
sustainability.csrFundsBody: We help partners structure, deploy, and track CSR funds for education — with governance, execution oversight, and impact reporting.

getInvolved.title: Get Involved
getInvolved.subtitle: Choose your pathway to make an impact.
getInvolved.reassurance: You can start small—membership, a one-time donation, or joining your local chapter.

items.a: Governance + chapters + digital platforms + media advocacy—integrated into one accountable system. We don't just fund projects; we build sustainable education ecosystems with measurable outcomes.

items.q: Can I support TVET or special needs education directly?
items.a: Yes. Choose a program and donate/partner to a specific track (TVET lab support or inclusive learning support). 100% of designated funds go to your chosen initiative with transparent reporting.
Yes—through structured local chapters and diaspora engagement. We operate in 54+ African countries and diaspora communities, each with local leadership aligned to SCEF governance standards.
Through detailed reports, digital updates via our Digital Board, and program dashboards. CSR partners receive quarterly impact statements and annual audited reports.

programs.title: Our 6 Programs
programs.intro: SCEF delivers impact through six flagship programs designed to work together on access, quality, inclusion, skills, and recognition.

cards.nesa: New Education Standard Award Africa (NESA Africa) — Recognizing and amplifying educational excellence.
cards.eduaid: Education Aid Africa (EduAid Africa) — Scholarships, support pathways, and education funding.
cards.rmsa: Rebuild My School Africa (RMSA) — School renewal and learning environment improvement.
cards.eoa: Education Online Africa (EOA) — Digital learning and verifiable certification.
cards.womenGirls: Women & Girls Education — Targeted interventions for access and outcomes.
cards.specialNeeds: Special Needs Education Support — Inclusion and support for learners.
cards.elibrary: eLibrary Nigeria — Free and curated learning resources.

eoaSpotlight.title: Education Online Africa (EOA): Learn. Get Certified. Renew.
eoaSpotlight.body: EOA helps learners build practical skills, earn verifiable credentials, and renew every 24 months. Pathways include ACDL and AWPC, plus an exam portal with integrity controls.

items.exec: Governance & Executive Office — Executive Secretary (Legal & Governance) + PRO
items.sobcd: SOBCD — Admin, HR, finance oversight, events, training, compliance
items.tdsd: TDSD — Web/App, CMS, database, security, analytics, wallet-ready systems
items.ombdd: OMBDD — Fundraising comms, sponsors, growth, partnership pipeline
items.lcs: Local Chapter Services Division (LCS) — Activation, support, governance, reporting

trust.title: Governance & Trust
trust.body: SCEF maintains structured governance and accountability through boards, policies, and transparent impact reporting.

history.title: Our History
history.body: SCEF has evolved over time into a pan-African membership-driven platform — supporting education systems through programs, media, local chapters, and accountable partnerships.

vision.title: Vision 2035
vision.body: By 2035, SCEF aims to support a continuously improving education standard across Africa — strengthening chapter action, inclusion, trusted certification, and ethical funding with credible impact reporting.

final.title: Be part of Africa's education transformation.
final.subtitle: Join the movement to make education accessible, inclusive, and impactful across the continent.

hero.title: Governed for Trust. Structured for Scale.
hero.subtitle: The Board Governance & Executive Office (BGEO) is the apex governance division providing institutional leadership, fiduciary oversight, and strategic direction for all SCEF operations across Africa.

bgeo.title: BGEO Division
bgeo.body: The Board Governance & Executive Office serves as the institutional nerve center, coordinating board activities, executive leadership, and ensuring SCEF delivers on its pan-African education mandate with integrity and accountability.
bgeo.whyBody: Africa's education sector has suffered from fragmented initiatives and weak oversight. SCEF's governance model provides clear accountability, transparent decision-making, and institutional continuity.

layers.title: Four-Layer Governance Structure
layers.subtitle: Each layer has clear mandates, accountability lines, and operational scope ensuring transparent and effective governance.

bot.title: Board of Trustees (BOT)
bot.description: The highest governing body responsible for fiduciary oversight, institutional integrity, and strategic direction of the Foundation.

boa.title: Boards of Advisors (BOA)
boa.description: Expert advisory panels providing specialized guidance on program delivery and chapter operations.

bod.title: Regional Boards of Directors (BOD)
bod.description: Operational governance ensuring regional program execution and adherence to institutional standards.

lcps.title: Local Chapter Presidents (LCPs)
lcps.description: On-ground leadership executing programs and managing community engagement at country level.

management.title: Executive Management Team
management.subtitle: SCEF's executive leadership oversees the six operational divisions, ensuring coordinated delivery of our pan-African education mandate.

orgSecretary.title: Organization Secretary
orgSecretary.description: Provides institutional leadership, legal counsel, and ensures governance compliance across all SCEF operations.

pro.title: Public Relations Officer (PRO)
pro.description: Manages institutional communications, media relations, and stakeholder engagement strategies.

operations.title: Director of Operations
operations.description: Oversees strategic operations, business compliance, and institutional risk management.

technology.title: Director of Technology
technology.description: Leads digital infrastructure, platform development, and technology-enabled education delivery.

mediaBusiness.title: Director of Media Business
mediaBusiness.description: Drives digital growth, sponsorship acquisition, and online brand development.

santosMedia.title: Director of Santos Media
santosMedia.description: Manages broadcasting, content production, and media platform operations.

chapterServices.title: Director of Chapter Services
chapterServices.description: Supports local chapters with onboarding, compliance, and operational guidance.

policies.title: Policies & Compliance
policies.subtitle: SCEF maintains comprehensive governance policies ensuring transparency, accountability, and ethical operations.

antiCorruption.desc: Zero tolerance for fraud and corruption
